chief_complaint: A 45-year-old male presented with a 2-day history of melena and hematemesis.
history: The patient reported a 2-week history of abdominal pain and weight loss. He had a history of iron deficiency anemia and was taking iron supplements. He had traveled to a rural area 6 months ago. He denied any history of gastrointestinal bleeding or other significant medical conditions.
exam: Vital signs: BP 120/80 mmHg, HR 100 bpm, RR 20/min. Physical examination revealed a pale complexion and a palpable spleen.
labs: Hemoglobin 8.5 g/dL, MCV 65 fL, MCH 20.5 pg, platelet count 150,000/μL. Stool examination revealed hookworm eggs.
